name: Email Adapter
description: One outbound-mail queue every block enqueues into (plan/vocabulary/01-email-adapter-spec.md), instead of each app reinventing an inline smtplib call that blocks the response, loses the message on any exception, and leaves no record a send was even attempted. A block calls object_email.enqueue(to, subject, text_body, ...) -- a plain local write of one queued email_outbox row -- and returns; the daemon's process_email_outbox pass renders each due row and hands it to the configured transport (DBBASIC_SMTP_MODE = disabled | log | live), recording the outcome back onto the row (sent / retry-with-backoff / dead). No page, no signed-in surface: this is a platform enabler for submit confirmations, notify's email channel, invoice delivery, membership invites, and rollup digests. The queue is admin-readable and closed to the generic POST write surface, so it can never be misconfigured into a public relay. SMTP credentials live in the deploy env (DBBASIC_SMTP_*), never a record or backup. email_enabled feature flag is the brownout kill switch. This package ships the schema + admin-read permission; the daemon pass and smtplib call live in core (object_email + object_daemon), there being no manifest hook to register a daemon pass yet.
